calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Continued To Jun-23-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Request And Motion For Prove Up Hearing Filed In Support Of Application For Default Judgment Against Defendants Francisco Magana And Deyanira Espinosa
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Francisco Magana, an individual and Deyanira Espinosa, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Marina Franco, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Central Apartments.
Ken Karp, Manager for plaintiff Central Apartments, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Ms. Franco and Mr. Karp appeared via ZOOM for today's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Francisco Magana, an individual or Deyanira Espinosa, an individual.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on June 22, 2020, and personally served on defendant Francisco Magana on July 17, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Deyanira Espinosa occurred on July 17, 2020, and was effectuated on July 27, 2020.
Default as to both defendants was entered on August 31, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on November 13, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Central Apartments, and against the defaulted defendants Francisco Magana, an individual and Deyanira Espinosa, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $19,156.56.
Costs - $990.00.

Total - $20,146.56.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Continued To Apr-21-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Reclassify Case From Limited Jurisdiction To Unlimited Jurisdiction Pursuant To Ccp Sect 403 040
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:
Marina Franco, Esq., (415) 567-9600, appearing for CENTRAL APARTMENTS,Attorney Ad-Litem.
No other appearance.

Hearing held. Ms. Franco stated that she did not contest the tentative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CENTRAL APARTMENTS Motion To Reclassify Case From Limited Jurisdiction To Unlimited Jurisdiction Pursuant To Ccp Sect 403 040 is GRANTED (subject to review of the order). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2020 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
